source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER

About Cambly: Our mission is to bring high-quality English education to every English learner in the world at the touch of a button. Think of us as the Airbnb that matches English students with English tutors. Come help us build the world's largest English learning marketplace and school.

We're looking for a Staff Software Engineer to own the technical direction of the products we ship. You'll be a senior technical owner on a fast-moving engineering team of around 15 engineers: backend-strong, comfortable working across the stack, and accountable for taking products from design through launch. You'll work closely with product managers, designers, and engineers, and you'll set the bar for how the team builds.

We're bringing AI to language learning in a way that strengthens what makes Cambly work: real tutors and real human connection. And we build AI-first: implementation is delegated to AI coding tools so engineers spend their time on the decisions, design, and review that actually shape the product. That's how you work too.

Our frontend is built on Next.js, React, and TypeScript. Our backend is built with Python (Flask and FastAPI) on AWS with MongoDB.



YOU WILL

 - Own the product platform. Lead the design and delivery of our backend systems: APIs, data models, and services, and be accountable for them working in production.

 - Make the hard calls. Own the judgment that shapes product work, including how we model the domain and when to build for scale and extensibility versus building quickly for fast learnings.

 - Ship end to end. Take products from technical design through launch, and own major initiatives across the team.

 - Work across the stack. Pick up frontend, mobile, and data work outside your core when a product needs it, using AI to be effective in areas where you aren't the resident expert.

 - Set how we build. Raise the technical bar through architecture, reviews, and good practices, and turn the AI workflows that work into practices the rest of the team adopts.

 - Grow the team. Mentor engineers, review their work, and help raise the standard of everyone around you.


WORK ON PROJECTS LIKE

 - Owning the platform behind a new AI-forward learning experience that generates personalized activities for each learner, and shaping it as we learn what works.

 - Designing the backend systems and data models that hold up as an AI-forward product grows.

 - Building post-lesson activities curated to each learner's areas for improvement.


ABOUT YOU

 - 7+ years of engineering experience, with a track record of owning systems and leading them to production.

 - Deep backend ability: API design, data modeling, and production systems that hold up at scale.

 - You build AI-first. Directing AI coding tools to do the implementation, and spending your own time on decisions and review, is already how you work, not something you're curious to try.

 - You own your work and have the technical judgment to make the calls that come with it, including when to invest in scale and extensibility and when to optimize for speed and learning.

 - Comfortable working outside your core. You'd rather pick up unfamiliar parts of the stack and ship than wait for a specialist.

 - A product mindset. You care about shipping things learners actually use.

 - A clear communicator who can explain technical trade-offs to engineers and non-engineers alike.


LIFE AT CAMBLY INCLUDES

 - $60M VC backed. Check out why Bessemer https://www.bvp.com/news/our-investment-in-cambly invested in us.

 - Hybrid first company. Office days are Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday.

 - Daily catered lunch.

 - 100% of medical, dental, and vision paid, along with 401k matching up to 4%.

 - Maternity and paternity paid leave.

 - 20 days of PTO, 9 days of sick leave, 10 company holidays, plus an end-of-year shutdown.

 - Helping people around the world unlock economic opportunities by learning English.


COMPENSATION

This role is based in

source_jobs.title: Sales Operations Manager - Brazil
source_jobs.description:
Cambly is one of the largest online English language platforms in the world, backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer and Benchmark. We help thousands of people improve their English and partner with hundreds of the world's most prestigious companies to empower their teams with language skills for global success.

We're looking for a technical, builder-minded Sales Operations Specialist/Manager to design and own the systems that power our B2B revenue engine.



WHY JOIN CAMBLY?

 - High-Impact Role: No inherited playbook — you'll architect sales infrastructure from the ground up and see your work shape the whole revenue team.

 - Career Growth: Direct exposure to sales leadership and the entire GTM stack, with room to grow into an ops leadership role as we scale.

 - Culture: Employee-first, results-focused, no unnecessary stress or nonsense.


THE ROLE

You'll be given a direction, not a spec. You're expected to figure out the "how," architect systems that haven't existed before, and ship them — stitching HubSpot, Clay, LinkedIn, call recording/conversation intelligence, and AI into a stack that actually works together.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and build sales workflows and tooling from scratch, often as the first person to solve a given problem at Cambly

 - Own HubSpot end-to-end: data model, pipeline stages, automations, reporting, integrations

 - Build and maintain prospecting/enrichment workflows using Clay, LinkedIn, and similar tools

 - Implement and optimize call recording/conversation intelligence tools for coaching and pipeline insight

 - Identify and build AI-enabled workflows across the sales cycle (lead scoring, call summarization, forecasting)

 - Build dashboards and reporting for real-time pipeline health, rep performance, and forecast accuracy

 - Diagnose bottlenecks in the sales cycle and design scalable processes, not one-off patches

 - Partner cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, and Finance to keep systems and data aligned


QUALIFICATIONS

Must-Haves

 - 6+ years in Sales/Revenue Operations, ideally at a fast-growing B2B SaaS company

 - Hands-on expertise with HubSpot, Clay, LinkedIn (Sales Navigator), and call recording/conversation intelligence tools

 - Practical, hands-on experience building with AI tools in a sales workflow

 - Track record of architecting systems or processes that didn't exist before, from a loose brief

 - Technical mindset: comfortable with data, integrations, automation logic, troubleshooting your own builds

 - Proactive, ownership-driven — defaults to action, iterates in the open

Preferred

 - Experience scaling a sales ops function at an early or growth-stage startup

 - Familiarity with SQL, APIs, or no-code/low-code tools (Zapier, Make)

 - Experience supporting both SDR and AE motions

 - Prior experience in a remote & distributed team

Skills & Mindset

 - Builder mentality — energized by ambiguity, not slowed by it

 - Strong organizational and project management skills across multiple systems/stakeholders

 - Clear communicator who can translate technical systems into simple guidance for reps and leadership

 - Comfortable in a fast-paced, dynamic, remote environment


WHAT YOU'LL ENJOY (BRAZIL)

 - Competitive salary and professional development budget

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and life insurance

 - Modern Health access: 6 free coaching + 6 free therapy sessions

 - Hybrid work; 30 calendar days of mandatory paid time off (after 12 months)

 - Meal voucher (Flash Card)

 - Employee-first, growth-focused culture

 - A truly global team

 - Free access to Cambly to improve your own language skills

Interested? Applying is simple: just click 'Apply' and upload your CV to get started.

source_jobs.title: Senior Designer - B2B & B2C
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CAMBLY

Cambly is transforming how the world learns English. With millions of students worldwide and thousands of native English-speaking tutors, we’ve built a platform where real conversations lead to real fluency. We’re not just another language app—we’re creating authentic human connections that break down barriers and open up opportunities.

As we continue expanding globally, we’re scaling our creative capabilities to support rapidly growing B2B initiatives and global B2C marketing campaigns across multiple regions and markets. This is where you come in.


ABOUT THE ROLE


We're seeking a talented, strategic Senior Marketing Designer to own creative production across Cambly's global B2B and B2C initiatives. You'll support B2B marketing and sales enablement (enterprise collateral, presentations, localized materials), collaborate on B2C campaigns and global initiatives, and support agile social media content.

This role requires fast execution, strong collaboration across teams, and the ability to scale creative output across global markets while maintaining brand consistency. You'll leverage design expertise, video production knowledge, and AI-assisted tools to improve both quality and efficiency.


What You'll Do

 - Design high-impact B2B assets: enterprise collateral, sales presentations, localized marketing materials, and client-facing deliverables

 - Support global B2C campaigns: digital ads, landing pages, email, and multi-channel campaign creative

 - Produce video and motion content: short-form video, motion graphics, animated presentations, and social-first video assets

 - Execute agile social production: plan and direct content shoots, capture testimonials and student stories, edit and adapt content for rapid iteration and daily delivery across platforms

 - Develop localized creative: adapt global campaigns across regions, languages, and cultural contexts; support regional initiatives and holidays

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: work with marketing, sales enablement, localization, content, and regional teams to deliver cohesive campaigns

 - Optimize workflows: help improve creative production processes, leveraging AI tools for ideation, layout generation, editing, and scalability

 - Stay ahead of trends: remain current on design, motion, social, and AI-assisted creative tools


REQUIREMENTS (MUST-HAVES)

 - 5+ years of design experience in B2B and/or B2C marketing environments

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating expertise in digital marketing, campaign, and presentation design

 - Strong English communication skills (written and spoken)

 - Proven video production and editing experience (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or equivalent)

 - Proficiency in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, and After Effects)

 - Strong visual composition, typography, animation, and storytelling skills

 - Experience creating social-first content, including video editing and content adaptation

 - Highly organized, proactive, and comfortable managing multiple fast-paced projects across teams and time zones

 - Comfortable operating in high-volume environments with rapid feedback and iteration cycles

 - Familiarity with AI-assisted creative tools for ideation, layout generation, image editing, or motion workflows

Nice-to-Have

 - Experience with AI-assisted creative tools (layout generation, image editing, motion workflows)

 - Familiarity with localization workflows and cross-cultural design

 - Experience designing for global brands or scaling startups

 - Photography or video direction experience for social-first content

Why You'll Love This Role

 - Impact at scale: your work directly supports enterprise growth and millions of learners globally

 - Creative variety: B2B enterprise materials, B2C campaigns, motion graphics, and social — never boring

 - End-to-end ownership: from strategy and creative direction to final production and optimization

 - Collaborative culture: work closely with mark
